description: Concluding a change helped from a single unseeded run when the effect is within run-to-run variance.
body: Failure mode: a config change is declared an improvement based on one run, but initialization and shuffling variance is as large as the claimed gain, so the conclusion is noise. Detection signals: no seed is set; re-running the baseline gives a different number than reported; the improvement is smaller than the spread across seeds. Do not compare single runs without controlling randomness. Safer fallback: fix seeds, run each config across several seeds, and require the mean difference to exceed the seed-to-seed standard deviation before claiming a win.
